titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Historical Picture Collection

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday February 12, 2020 at 08:57
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.abstract: This collection consists of daguerreotypes, ambrotypes, tintypes, miniature paintings, and other direct positive photographic and non-photographic pictures, brought together here from numerous collections for special preservation and protection. Many of these pictures were formerly listed as part of the Yale Objects and Yale Picture Collections. Others were removed from various collections of personal and family papers: George Kittredge Collection, Gilman Family Papers, Chauncey Family Papers, Leicester Ely Family Papers, Page Family Papers, Wickham Family Papers, Baldwin Family Papers, Bidwell Family Papers, Bingham Family Papers, Brewer Family Papers, Hiram Bingham II Papers, Beecher Family Papers, the Roe Family Papers, Charles Lindbergh Papers, William Henry Brewer Papers, Tarleton Family Papers, Lyman Family Papers, and William Graham Sumner Papers. Most of the pictures are formal or semi-formal portraits of individuals or groups; most are daguerreotypes. Exact information about the dates is usually not available, but most of these pictures were produced in the 1840s and 1850s. Among the portraits of particular interest – historical and aesthetic – are those of Henry Ward Beecher, John C. Calhoun, Josiah Willard Gibbs, Denison Olmsted, and Benjamin Silliman.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown, though much of the material in this collection is likely in the public domain. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was organized to provide each item optimum storage protection. Since this has in some cases necessitated dispersing groups of pictures according to size and type, supplementary information is provided in theguide, though it is scanty in most cases. The pictures were assigned item numbers which correspond to numbered slots or envelopes in the storage boxes: numbers preceded by "A" refer to pictures given flat storage, and "B" to vertical storage.

Additions to this collection include accessions of photographic and pictorial material, and item or items transferred from manuscript and archival collections due to their sensitive physical state and value. Accessions or transfers of material to this collection must possess sufficient historical signifiance, through provenance or content to warrant addition.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Historical Picture Collection (MS 713). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection consists of daguerreotypes, ambrotypes, tintypes, miniature paintings, and other direct positive photographic and non-photographic pictures, brought together here from numerous collections for special preservation and protection. Many of these pictures were formerly listed as part of the Yale Objects and Yale Picture Collections. Others were removed from various collections of personal and family papers: George Kittredge Collection, Gilman Family Papers, Chauncey Family Papers, Leicester Ely Family Papers, Page Family Papers, Wickham Family Papers, Baldwin Family Papers, Bidwell Family Papers, Bingham Family Papers, Brewer Family Papers, Hiram Bingham II Papers, Beecher Family Papers, the Roe Family Papers, Charles Lindbergh Papers, William Henry Brewer Papers, Tarleton Family Papers, Lyman Family Papers, and William Graham Sumner Papers. Most of the pictures are formal or semi-formal portraits of individuals or groups; most are daguerreotypes. Exact information about the dates is usually not available, but most of these pictures were produced in the 1840s and 1850s. Among the portraits of particular interest – historical and aesthetic – are those of Henry Ward Beecher, John C. Calhoun, Josiah Willard Gibbs, Denison Olmsted, and Benjamin Silliman.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: The Todd-Bingham family images were transferred to the Todd-Bingham Picture Collection (MS 496E).
The pictures of Yale individuals from classes were transferred to Yale Classes Photographs (RU 779).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in two series: I. Miscellaneous photographs, drawings, etchings, engravings, and other prints, 1780-1975. II. Daguerreotypes and other early photographs, miniature paintings and other nonphotographic pictures, circa 1842-circa 1872.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous photographs, drawings, etchings, engravings, and other prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I consists of miscellaneous photographs, drawings, etchings, engravings and other prints. There are also several albums of photographs and rotogravure sections of newspapers kept in the collection for their photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 71 mounted photographs, 26x34 centimeters, lacquered wood covers. 52 of the photographs are hand tinted views of Japan and Japanese daily life. The remainder are of missionaries of Haoke, 1891; views of Kanazawa, the College, Boys' School (1897), Missionary Residence, Azabu Girls' School, Tokyo, circa 1900; School at Shiznoka, 1913; United Council Meeting, Kamakura, 1928.

did.unittitle: Album of photographs relating to the "Insurgent Army"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Philippine Islands, 23 photographs mounted on 17 leaves; 3 photographs laid in. Shown are photographs of officers and events related to the "Insurgent Army" [circa 1896], a public execution, municipal buildings and scenes of daily life, largely in the town of Vigan. Among the officers identified are Memerto Natividad and Manuel Tinio.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: News photographs of individuals and events in the 1930s in eighteen European countries, the United States, and the Far East. The largest groups of photographs relate to political events in Austria and Germany. See box 21a for copies of Viennese newspapers in which some of these photographs originally appeared, contains 253 items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 8 photographs of Delaware and Hudson Company headquarters buildings, yards, canals, aqueducts, and of Leonor Fresnel Loree (1858-1940), president of the Delaware and Hudson Company, 1907-1938; 4 photographs of the track of the Gravity Railroad and a "Profile of the Gravity Railroad"; 5 photographs of old railroad cars, among them the "Stourbridge Lion," a car on the Gravity Road; 2 maps of the Delaware and Hudson Canal Company's transportation system, 1866; and 1 photograph of a portrait of Philip Hone (1780-1851), a supporter of the Delaware and Hudson canal. From the Brown Collection, Harvard University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of engravings, cartes de visites, and photographs largely of 19th century English, French, and German scientists. In the collection are 23 engravings of European and American scientists who worked in the fields of chemistry and agricultural research; 44 photographs of 19th century German scientists; and 7 cartes de visites of English and German 19th century scientists.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 88 items; a miscellaneous collection of 19th century engravings, some taken from books and magazines. The collection includes pictures on American Indian life, views of European and American cities, including several early views of New York, portraits, largely of figures associated with the American Revolution, and engravings of paintings and sculptures.

did.unittitle: Near East - Social life and customs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of four photographs, sepia, of life in the Near East: one street scene showing a man surrounded by dogs, titled: "Les chiens errant de Cana," 25x20 centimeters; one view of three veiled women and a child picnicking in an open field by the side of a carriage, 19x25 centimeters; seven elderly men in ragged clothes, posed in a studio [?] with pottery in the foreground, 19x26 centimeters; and one seated male figure with staff, posed in a studio [?], 25x19 centimeters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 402 black and white photographs of 28 battle sites in Spain during the Spanish Civil War (1936-1939) together with pictures of General Francisco Franco, Spanish officers, German and Italian forces and miscellaneous street scenes. The photographs were probably taken, mounted, and annotated by a member of a German photographic unit. Provenance of the papers is unknown.

odd.head: General

did.unittitle: View of Spain from a German military ship
German armed forces - On the march

Edward VIII, King of Great Britain (1894-1972)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 187 photographs of Edward VIII from infancy until 1914, showing him alone, with members of the royal family, and as a participant in various official events. Among the early pictures are several with Queen Victoria and Edward VII. He is also shown with his immediate family: King George V and Queen Mary, and his brothers and sister.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 72 photographs; a collection of seventy-two views of pre-1914 Hungary, of which fifty-seven are by Vágó and the remainder by other photographers from Budapest. Many of the scenes are from areas of the Austro-Hungarian empire now part of Czechoslovakia, Romania, the Soviet Union, and Yugoslavia. In addition to landscapes, views of towns, and stretches of railroad track, there are pictures of peasants in local costume and gypsies in their encampments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 1 black and white photograph containing signatures of James C. [Benedum?] (b. 1909, involved in oil industry), Joseph H. Willits (1880-1979, Director of Social Sciences, Rockefeller Foundation), Wayne L. Morse (1900-1974, senator, arbitrator), Huston Thompson, and Thomas Reed Powell (1880-1955, lawyer, served on the President's Emergency Board on the National Railroad Strike in 1941). Received from Arts of the Book, 1994 Jul 18.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains daguerreotypes and other early photographs, miniature paintings and other direct positive photographic and non-photographic pictures. Included in this series are pictures of Yale men from the classes of 1848 to 1858 and pictures removed from collections held in Manuscripts and Archives.

scopecontent.p: Consists of a cased ambrotype, 9x9.5 centimeters, "A.P. Critchlow & Co., Manufacturers of Daguerreotype Cases. A.P.C. & Co. are the Original Inventors of the Composition for the Union Case, (so called,) including all the various shades of color and finenes of texture peculiar to their manufacture and of the Embracing Rivited Hinges, thus securing them from breaking out as do others that are inserted with or without a metal brace." printed on a paper attached to the case behind the plate, "This is F.M. Adams Yale 1841 and was copied by Bundy & Filley in cabinet size and is in the album with most of the pictures of that size -- cost of copy $1.50" handwritten on a paper formerly enclosed in the case. Gift of Clara L. Barnum.

did.unittitle: Eggleston, captain Jack and Sarah (Mrs. Jack)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of a cased ambrotype, 9.5x12 centimeters, "This is a picture of Captain Jack Eggleston and his wife, Sarah. They were intimate friends of both my father and mother. There is an amusing description in my father's letter of April 3, 1865, of a fight which had been witnessed by Captain Eggleston. Captain Eggleston was a captain in the navy" typed on a paper formerly enclosed in the case. Removed from Tarleton Family Papers.
